name: Tomates Yemistes (Stuffed Tomatoes)
recipeInstructions:
Serves: 6 Cooking time: 45 minutes Oven temperature: 180°C (350°F)
1. Slice the tops off the tomatoes and set aside.
2. Scoop out the pulp from the tomatoes and sprinkle with a little sugar. Set aside.
3. In a saucepan, combine the tomato pulp, salt, pepper, and 1/2 teaspoon of sugar. Simmer until soft.
4. Press the mixture through a sieve and set aside.
5. In a large skillet, gently fry onion in half of the oil until transparent. Add pine nuts (if using) and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
6. Stir in rice, currants, hot water, parsley, mint, and season to taste. Bring to a boil, cover, and simmer gently for 10 minutes until the liquid is absorbed.
7. Fill each tomato with the rice mixture, leaving room for expansion. Replace the tops on the tomatoes and place them in an oven dish.
8. Pour the pureed tomato pulp and an equal quantity of water or white wine into the dish. Spoon any remaining oil over the tomatoes.
9. Cook uncovered in a moderate oven for 30 minutes. Serve hot or cold.
Note: This makes a pleasant luncheon dish or an attractive accompaniment to main meals.
